dsl_rule.translation_notes: Mean reversion strategy using one-day prior returns. Entry conditions use 1-day returns (the previous bar's return): buy when returns < 0 (worst performers), short when returns > 0 (best performers). Exit conditions are reversed positions (symmetrical reversal), typical for mean reversion. Hold period set to 1 day as per lookback_period. No stop loss specified by author. Percentile ranking cannot be precisely quantified without additional data on the full universe distribution, so we use return sign as proxy for worst/best performers.

backtest_rule.reason: Mean reversion strategy using one-day prior returns. Entry conditions use 1-day returns (the previous bar's return): buy when returns < 0 (worst performers), short when returns > 0 (best performers). Exit conditions are reversed positions (symmetrical reversal), typical for mean reversion. Hold period set to 1 day as per lookback_period. No stop loss specified by author. Percentile ranking cannot be precisely quantified without additional data on the full universe distribution, so we use return sign as proxy for worst/best performers.

source_rule_text: A mean reversion strategy that buys stocks with the worst previous one-day returns and shorts stocks with the best previous one-day returns.
